review_title: Strong Value, Flexible Automation, and Reliable Support
description: Overall, my experience with ACCELQ has been positive. It provides a strong platform for building and managing automated tests, especially for complex end-to-end workflows. The flexibility and customization options make it adaptable to different project needs, and it significantly reduces the effort required to scale automation. The support team has been a key strength, consistently responsive and helpful in resolving issues and guiding implementations. While there are some limitations in advanced scenarios and debugging can occasionally be challenging, the overall value, ease of use, and ability to handle real-world use cases make ACCELQ a reliable choice for test automation.
author: Akhila K.
pros: What I liked most about ACCELQ is the strong value it delivers for the cost. It is highly customizable, which makes it adaptable to different testing needs and workflows. The platform handles complex use cases effectively, especially for end-to-end scenarios. Additionally, the support team is very responsive and helpful, which makes a big difference when dealing with challenges or scaling usage.
cons: One of the limitations I experienced with ACCELQ is around flexibility in certain advanced scenarios, where customization sometimes requires workarounds instead of straightforward solutions. There are also occasional challenges with dynamic element handling and stability in complex test flows. Additionally, some integrations and features feel less mature, and debugging failures can take more effort than expected due to limited visibility in certain areas.
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: Easy setup, catching the bugs early and both frontend and backend tests run locally without hassle
description: We needed both frontend and backend testing, and all the scheduled tests needed to run locally on our own servers due to safety concerns for customer data, and AccelQ could give us that. Whenever I've run into problems or needed assistance on how to solve a task, I've always gotten quick help from support to find a solution. Scheduled tests are predictable, and we are catching more bugs than before at an earlier stage, with an average of 1-3 per week. More expensive than the others, and that was a discussion that almost changed the decision at first, but after I started using it, I do not regret the decision.
author: Anniken L.
pros: Been easy to learn, and little technical insight is needed to also cover more detailed and backend testing on my own with predefined commands. It's been great to be able to cover also more detailed and backend testing without involving developers for some additional code. The overview of the tests are clear and readable, and I like the easy switch between recording and not recording to be able to better change where in the test I need to continue working
cons: I'm missing more flexibility in views to work with and compare more in parallel to work faster. Also missing more focus on time management of the tests, but have hopes that the new dashboard with it's flexibility in setup also will cover this over time.
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: Solid Automation Tool That's Easy to Pick Up
description: Overall AccelQ has been a good fit for our test automation needs. It's user friendly and doesn't take long to get the hang of, which makes onboarding a lot smoother. The low-code setup lowers the barrier to entry and the support has been reliable when we've needed it.
author: Michael P.
pros: AccelQ has been a solid tool for building out test automation without needing to write a ton of code. The codeless/low-code approach is user friendly and pretty quick to pick up — you don't need a heavy technical background to get moving. The support team is responsive and helpful when you run into issues.
cons: Like any automation tool, you'll run into random failures that aren't really AccelQ's fault — flaky behavior during playback that can be hard to pin down. It's not a dealbreaker, just part of the territory with test automation in general.
author_duration: Used the software for: 2+ years

review_title: ACCELQ Has Completely Simplified Our Testing and Boosted Efficiency at Communication Technology Services
description: ACCELQ has been a perfect fit for Communication Technology Services. It’s helped us automate our testing processes efficiently, reduce manual errors, and improve the quality of every project we deliver. The platform has enhanced productivity across our QA and development teams and given us more confidence in our software performance across devices. Between the exceptional customer service, ease of use, and strong automation capabilities, ACCELQ has proven to be one of the best investments we’ve made. I would absolutely recommend it to any telecom company looking for a reliable and intelligent automated testing solution.
author: john T.
pros: At Communication Technology Services (CTS), we handle complex telecommunications systems where precision and reliability are absolutely critical. ACCELQ has made a huge difference in how we approach software testing across our internal tools and customer-facing applications. What I love most is how user-friendly and intuitive the platform is our team quickly adapted with almost no steep learning curve. The automation flows are smooth, and the ability to test across multiple devices has helped us ensure consistent performance for all our telecom clients. The real-time dashboards and project management capabilities keep everyone on the same page, and we’ve been able to shorten release cycles significantly. The value for money is exceptional, and the customer support is truly outstanding every time we’ve had a question, their team responded quickly, professionally, and with real solutions that made an impact.
cons: There’s honestly nothing to dislike. Every feature we’ve used has worked exactly as promised, and the ACCELQ team continuously provides great support and product improvements.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

cons: We initially struggled with organizing our test assets as the project grew. We had to learn and implement their folder and tagging structure to keep everything manageable, which took a little time.
author_profession: Creative Studio Operating Specialist and Manager Senior Product Artists
